content.text: Mastra is a TypeScript-first framework for building AI agents, structured multi-step workflows, and RAG pipelines. It is designed for teams already working in Node.js or serverless environments who want a single library that covers agents, memory, vector search, evaluations, and third-party integrations without switching languages.
as its model abstraction layer, so any model supported by the AI SDK — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, and others — works with Mastra without additional adapters.

attrs.alt: Mastra architecture: Mastra instance connecting agents, workflows, tools, memory, RAG, evals, and integrations

content.text: The quickest way to start is the scaffold CLI. To add Mastra to an existing Node.js project, install the core package:

npm create mastra@latest
# or add to an existing project:
npm install @mastra/core
Mastra requires Node.js 18+ and TypeScript 5+. The scaffold generates a
entry point, a sample agent, and a dev server configuration.
combines a model, instructions, and optional tools. Register agents with the
instance so they can be resolved by name across your application:

and Zod schemas for both input and output. Mastra validates the model's tool-call arguments against the input schema at runtime, preventing malformed calls from reaching your execute function:

and to surface schema information to evals and tracing tools.
Mastra's workflow engine models multi-step processes as a DAG of
objects. Each step declares its output schema; subsequent steps retrieve prior results via
after assembling the workflow to freeze the graph and validate connections. Then create a run with
and start it with initial input.
routes execution to the first step whose condition predicate returns
. Unlike simple if-else logic in a step, branches are statically declared in the graph and visible to Mastra's observability tooling:
Multiple branches can run in parallel if they are not mutually exclusive — use
for steps that have no dependency on each other.
class gives agents persistent, per-user conversation history. It combines a flat message store (for recency) with a vector store (for semantic recall of older messages):
Inject the N most recent messages into every request
Retrieve the K semantically closest older messages
Identifies the conversation thread (one per chat session)
Identifies the user or resource owning the thread
abstraction that wraps Postgres pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, and others behind a unified
upserts vectors with metadata into the named index.
performs approximate nearest-neighbour search and returns the top-K results with their metadata. Attach the vector store to an agent's tools to give it retrieval capability.
Mastra ships built-in LLM-as-judge evaluators for common quality dimensions. Call
in tests or CI to score agent outputs automatically:
Presence of harmful, offensive, or dangerous content
Factual alignment between output and reference summary
Whether output stays faithful to the retrieved context (RAG)
How relevant the output is to the input question
Signal-to-noise ratio of the retrieved RAG context

Comparing Mastra to Python Agent Frameworks

functions run server-side. Never place browser APIs, DOM access, or client-only imports inside them.

Mastra has a growing catalogue of first-party integration packages for GitHub, Slack, Notion, Jira, and others. Each integration exposes its REST API as a set of Mastra tools auto-generated from the provider's OpenAPI specification:
Using integration-generated tools means you never write boilerplate HTTP clients — Mastra handles auth, pagination, and schema validation automatically. The agent sees one tool per API operation.
Mastra supports OpenTelemetry out of the box. Pass an
constructor to trace every agent call, tool execution, and workflow step to any OTLP-compatible backend:
Traces include the full prompt sent to the model, token counts, tool arguments and results, and step durations — all correlated by a single trace ID that spans multi-step workflows.
Workflows can pause mid-execution to wait for human input. The
function serialises the current workflow state and returns a handle. Call
with the step ID and payload to continue:

The workflow state is persisted to storage while suspended, so the process can restart between suspension and resumption. This is the correct pattern for approval gates, manual review steps, and async webhooks.
Mastra is a TypeScript-native framework covering agents, DAG workflows, memory, RAG, and evaluations in a single library.
+ Zod schemas define tools with validated inputs and outputs.
persists conversation history per user (thread + resource) with both recency and semantic recall.
abstracts multiple vector backends behind a unified sync/query interface.
with LLM-as-judge metrics enables automated quality assurance in CI.
Mastra uses Vercel AI SDK for model support, making it straightforward to switch LLM providers without framework changes.
